01 — Selected Work

Projects

Six projects spanning spacecraft systems, propulsion, autonomous robotics, and mechatronic prototyping. Each combines hands-on hardware with electronics, sensing, and control.

P-01 Spacecraft Systems · Published

Attitude Determination & Rotational Test System

Undergraduate Researcher — Dr. Adam Huang / Aug 2023 – Apr 2026

Designed and built a rotational test system that interfaces with LabVIEW to command and manipulate the orientation of a small satellite, creating a controllable benchtop testbed for spacecraft attitude experiments. Developed the accompanying attitude determination system using PIC microcontrollers and MEMS inertial sensors to estimate orientation to high accuracy.

PIC microcontrollersMEMS sensorsLabVIEWPICBASICSensor fusionSolidWorks
▸Published as first author (AIAA 2025-98578) and presented at the AIAA 2024 & 2025 Regional Student Conferences.
P-02 Fluid Systems · NASA

Autonomous Commodity Management

Engineering Intern — NASA Stennis Space Center / Summer 2025

Designed a tabletop fluid-transfer system as an early prototype for a Martian propellant transfer architecture. Integrated fluid, mechanical, and electrical subsystems to move fluid between two tanks through an intermediate transfer tank. Coordinated actuation, sensing, and control into a single working demonstrator.

System integrationFluid systemsSensors & actuatorsControlPrototyping
▸Delivered a working benchtop prototype under advisors Travis Martin and Dr. John Schmalzel.
P-03 Robotics · Autonomy

Autonomous Quadrupedal Navigation

REU Researcher — Kent State University, Dr. Rui Liu / Summer 2024

Implemented a LiDAR-based SLAM pipeline enabling real-time collision detection and avoidance on a quadruped robot. Paired the mapping system with object tracking and localization so the platform could navigate and act fully autonomously.

LiDAR SLAMPythonPerceptionLocalizationControls
▸Presented at the Northeast Ohio REU Symposium (poster), Kent, OH.
P-04 Propulsion · Founder

Hybrid Rocket Test Stand

Founder & Project Lead — Univ. of Arkansas / Sep 2024 – Apr 2026

Founded a student rocket team and built a propulsion test stand that opened up undergraduate propulsion research at the University of Arkansas. Piloted the stand's first research campaign, investigating joule-heated fuel grains for controlled regression-rate studies.

Test stand designData acquisitionInstrumentationPropulsionFabrication
▸Established a lasting research capability and an active undergraduate research program.
P-05 Aerostructures · Leadership

Design / Build / Fly

Lead Engineer 2025–26 · Member 2024–25 / Aug 2024 – Apr 2026

Led engineering for a 21-member team designing and building a radio-controlled aircraft for the AIAA Design/Build/Fly competition, directing the mechanical design effort across missions. As a junior, personally led the design for one competition mission.

SolidWorksFEAAerostructuresSystems engineeringTeam leadership
▸1st place in one of three scored missions, and 5th in the world for the mission led as a junior.
P-06 Plasma · Experimental

Plasma-Induced Ionic Flight

Undergraduate Researcher — Dr. Adam Huang / Summer 2023

Investigated ion-driven propulsion (ionic flight) and worked to optimize thrust generation using plasma. Tested a range of dielectric thicknesses to qualitatively characterize their effect on plasma generation.

Plasma physicsHigh-voltage electronicsExperimental designData analysis
▸Early-stage characterization informing follow-on high-voltage propulsion work.

04 — Profile

About

I'm a mechanical / aerospace engineer that graduated from the University of Arkansas in May 2026 with a dual degree, Summa Cum Laude, and a 3.92 GPA.

My focus is mechanical and aerospace design, and I like problems that fuse structure, sensing, and control into one product. I've built spacecraft attitude hardware, a hybrid-rocket propulsion test stand, a fluid-transfer prototype at NASA, and autonomy for a quadruped robot, and I enjoy owning a build end to end, from CAD and machining through test and data.
